channel.title: AI Unfiltered
channel.description: Chinese AI • Open Source • Security • Incidents. Signal, not noise.

item.title: ChatGPT free users are seeing errors when having conversations
item.description: Status: MonitoringWe have applied the mitigation and are monitoring the recovery.Affected components Conversations (Partial outage)

item.title: Zibian Robotics Launches WALL-B Embodied AI Model for Home Deployment
item.description: Zibian Robotics introduced WALL-B, a next-gen embodied AI model designed for real-world home deployment with multimodal learning and physical reasoning.

item.title: Ant Digital Technologies Launches AI Payment Feature on DTClaw Platform
item.description: Ant Digital Technologies launched AI-powered payments on DTClaw, enabling autonomous agents to complete transactions via Alipay with built-in security controls.

item.title: CATL Unveils Third-Generation Qilin Battery with Ultra-Fast Charging and 1,000 km Range
item.description: CATL unveiled its third-gen Qilin battery with ultra-fast charging and over 1,000 km range, pushing EV performance and efficiency to new levels.

item.title: Robotic Joint Developer Quanzhibo Raises Hundreds of Millions of RMB in Series A++ Round
item.description: Chinese robotics firm Quanzhibo raised tens of millions USD in fresh funding to scale production of high-performance robot joints for humanoid and quadruped robots.

item.title: Moonshot AI Open-Sources Kimi K2.6, Advancing Multi-Agent Collaboration
item.description: Moonshot AI has open-sourced its Kimi K2.6 model, introducing stronger multi-agent collaboration and matching top closed-source models in key benchmarks.

item.title: [AINews] OpenAI launches GPT-Image-2
item.description: with Cursor getting a $10B contract with xAI and a right to acquire for $60B.

item.title: Roundtables: Unveiling The 10 Things That Matter in AI Right Now
item.description: Listen to the session or watch below Watch a special edition of Roundtables simulcast live from EmTech AI, MIT Technology Review’s signature conference for AI leadership. Subscribers got an exclusive first look at a new list capturing 10 key technologies, emerging trends, bold ideas, and powerful...

item.title: The new word in home construction could be “plastics”
item.description: Single-use plastics are a persistent source of environmental pollution, and the need to house a growing global population puts increasing pressure on resources such as timber. MIT engineers have an idea that could make a dent in both problems at once. In a recent study, a team led by mechanical...

item.title: A natural protein may protect the GI tract from infection
item.description: Embedded in the body’s mucosal surfaces, proteins called lectins bind to sugars found on cell surfaces. A team led by MIT chemistry professor Laura Kiessling has found that one such protein, intelectin-2, both helps fortify the mucosal barrier and offers broad-spectrum protection against harmful...

item.title: Page Rules with Host Header Override issues
item.description: Apr 21, 20:19 UTCResolved - This incident has been resolved.Apr 21, 19:57 UTCMonitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results.Apr 21, 18:09 UTCIdentified - Cloudflare has identified an issue with customers creating/updating Page Rules with the Host Header Override setting....

item.title: Cloudflare Gateway HTTP Logs issues
item.description: Apr 21, 19:36 UTCInvestigating - Cloudflare is observing issues with Cloudflare Gateway HTTP Logs service that may delay log ingestion.

item.title: Investigating potential impact to Realtime services in Bombay
item.description: Apr 21, 18:37 UTCMonitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results.Apr 21, 18:30 UTCIdentified - The issue has been identified and a fix is being implemented.Apr 21, 17:46 UTCInvestigating - We are currently investigating reports of degraded Realtime service in the Mumbai...

item.title: SystemBC C2 Server Reveals 1,570+ Victims in The Gentlemen Ransomware Operation
item.description: Threat actors associated with The Gentlemen ransomware‑as‑a‑service (RaaS) operation have been observed attempting to deploy a known proxy malware called SystemBC. According to new research published by Check Point, the command-and-control (C2 or C&C) server linked to SystemBC has led to the...

item.title: Building agent-first governance and security
item.description: As AI agents increasingly work alongside humans across organizations, companies could be inadvertently opening a new attack surface. Insecure agents can be manipulated to access sensitive systems and proprietary data, increasing enterprise risk. In some modern enterprises, non-human identities...

item.title: Log Explorer Issues
item.description: Apr 21, 16:49 UTCResolved - This incident has been resolved.Apr 21, 16:31 UTCMonitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results.Apr 21, 16:25 UTCIdentified - The issue has been identified and a fix is being implemented.Apr 21, 15:02 UTCInvestigating - Cloudflare is...

item.title: Disruption with projects service
item.description: Apr 21, 16:45 UTCUpdate - The mitigation is deployed and we are seeing recorvery in the queues and will provide an update as to when full recovery will be realized.Apr 21, 16:18 UTCUpdate - We are deploying a fix to relieve the queue of delayed data. Some users may still experience delays with...

item.title: Cloudflare Pages build issues
item.description: Apr 21, 16:07 UTCIdentified - The issue has been identified and a fix is being implemented.Apr 21, 15:46 UTCInvestigating - Cloudflare is aware of and investigating an issue with Cloudflare Pages builds which potentially impacts multiple customers. Users may not be able to push changes to their...

item.title: 22 BRIDGE:BREAK Flaws Expose Thousands of Lantronix and Silex Serial-to-IP Converters
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have identified 22 new vulnerabilities in popular models of serial-to-IP converters from Lantronix and Silex that could be exploited to hijack susceptible devices and tamper with data exchanged by them. The vulnerabilities have been collectively codenamed BRIDGE:BREAK by...

item.title: ‘Scattered Spider’ Member ‘Tylerb’ Pleads Guilty
item.description: A 24-year-old British national and senior member of the cybercrime group "Scattered Spider" has pleaded guilty to wire fraud conspiracy and aggravated identity theft. Tyler Robert Buchanan admitted his role in a series of text-message phishing attacks in the summer of 2022 that allowed the group to...

item.title: Third US Security Expert Admits Helping Ransomware Gang
item.description: Angelo Martino of Florida has pleaded guilty to collaborating with the BlackCat cybercrime group while working as a ransomware negotiator. The post Third US Security Expert Admits Helping Ransomware Gang appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Ransomware Negotiator Pleads Guilty to Aiding BlackCat Attacks in 2023
item.description: A third individual who was employed as a ransomware negotiator has pleaded guilty to conducting ransomware attacks against U.S. companies in 2023. Angelo Martino, 41, of Land O'Lakes, Florida, teamed up with the operators of the BlackCat ransomware starting in April 2023 to assist the e-crime gang...

item.title: Dozens of Malicious Crypto Apps Land in Apple App Store
item.description: Masquerading as popular cryptocurrency wallets, the apps can hijack recovery phrases and private keys. The post Dozens of Malicious Crypto Apps Land in Apple App Store appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: 5 Places where Mature SOCs Keep MTTR Fast and Others Waste Time
item.description: Security teams often present MTTR as an internal KPI. Leadership sees it differently: every hour a threat dwells inside the environment is an hour of potential data exfiltration, service disruption, regulatory exposure, and brand damage.  The root cause of slow MTTR is almost never "not enough...

item.title: NGate Campaign Targets Brazil, Trojanizes HandyPay to Steal NFC Data and PINs
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a new iteration of an Android malware family called NGate that has been found to abuse a legitimate application called HandyPay instead of NFCGate. "The threat actors took the app, which is used to relay NFC data, and patched it with...

item.title: Unsecured Perforce Servers Expose Sensitive Data From Major Orgs
item.description: Things are improving, but a researcher has still identified over 1,500 Perforce P4 instances allowing attackers to read files on the server. The post Unsecured Perforce Servers Expose Sensitive Data From Major Orgs appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Progress Patches Multiple Vulnerabilities in MOVEit WAF, LoadMaster
item.description: The security defects could be exploited for remote code execution, OS command injection, and WAF detection bypass. The post Progress Patches Multiple Vulnerabilities in MOVEit WAF, LoadMaster appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: The Download: turning down human noise, and LA’s stunning subway upgrade
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. The noise we make is hurting animals. Can we learn to shut up? As human society has expanded, animals have started struggling to hear one another....

item.title: New cross domain guidance for government, industry and the wider security community
item.description: Ensuring cross domain technologies are better understood - and more easily deployed - across sectors.

item.title: Cyber chief: UK faces "perfect storm" for cyber security
item.description: As the technology landscape develops, the definition of cyber security is expanding with it.

item.title: Organizations Warned of Exploited Cisco, Kentico, Zimbra Vulnerabilities
item.description: CISA expanded the KEV catalog with eight flaws, but five of them have been flagged as exploited before. The post Organizations Warned of Exploited Cisco, Kentico, Zimbra Vulnerabilities appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: QIMMA قِمّة ⛰: A Quality-First Arabic LLM Leaderboard
KWBench: Measuring Unprompted Problem Recognition in Knowledge Work
item.description: arXiv:2604.15760v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We introduce the first version of KWBench (Knowledge Work Bench), a benchmark for unprompted problem recognition in large language models: can an LLM identify a professional scenario before attempting to solve it. Existing frontier benchmarks have...

item.title: Stein Variational Black-Box Combinatorial Optimization
item.description: arXiv:2604.15837v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Combinatorial black-box optimization in high-dimensional settings demands a careful trade-off between exploiting promising regions of the search space and preserving sufficient exploration to identify multiple optima. Although...

item.title: Discover and Prove: An Open-source Agentic Framework for Hard Mode Automated Theorem Proving in Lean 4
item.description: arXiv:2604.15839v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Most ATP benchmarks embed the final answer within the formal statement -- a convention we call "Easy Mode" -- a design that simplifies the task relative to what human competitors face and may lead to optimistic estimates of model capability. We call...

item.title: Experience Compression Spectrum: Unifying Memory, Skills, and Rules in LLM Agents
item.description: arXiv:2604.15877v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: As LLM agents scale to long-horizon, multi-session deployments, efficiently managing accumulated experience becomes a critical bottleneck. Agent memory systems and agent skill discovery both address this challenge -- extracting reusable knowledge from...

item.title: Towards Rigorous Explainability by Feature Attribution
item.description: arXiv:2604.15898v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: For around a decade, non-symbolic methods have been the option of choice when explaining complex machine learning (ML) models. Unfortunately, such methods lack rigor and can mislead human decision-makers. In high-stakes uses of ML, the lack of rigor...

item.title: BASIS: Balanced Activation Sketching with Invariant Scalars for "Ghost Backpropagation"
item.description: arXiv:2604.16324v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The activation memory required for exact backpropagation scales linearly with network depth, context length, and feature dimensionality, forming an O(L * BN ) spatial bottleneck (where B is the sequence-batch cardinality and N is the feature...

item.title: UniMamba: A Unified Spatial-Temporal Modeling Framework with State-Space and Attention Integration
item.description: arXiv:2604.16325v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Multivariate time series forecasting is fundamental to numerous domains such as energy, finance, and environmental monitoring, where complex temporal dependencies and cross-variable interactions pose enduring challenges. Existing Transformer-based...

item.title: Annotation Entropy Predicts Per-Example Learning Dynamics in LoRA Fine-Tuning
item.description: arXiv:2604.16332v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We find that LoRA fine-tuning exhibits un-learning on contested examples: items with high annotator disagreement show increasing loss during training, a qualitatively distinct pattern largely absent under full fine-tuning and consistent across all six...

item.title: A Discordance-Aware Multimodal Framework with Multi-Agent Clinical Reasoning
item.description: arXiv:2604.16333v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Knee osteoarthritis frequently exhibits discordance between structural damage observed in imaging and patient-reported symptoms such as pain. This mismatch complicates clinical interpretation and patient stratification and remains insufficiently...

item.title: Preventing overfitting in deep learning using differential privacy
item.description: arXiv:2604.16334v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The use of Deep Neural Network based systems in the real world is growing. They have achieved state-of-the-art performance on many image, speech and text datasets. They have been shown to be powerful systems that are capable of learning detailed...

item.title: Multimodal Claim Extraction for Fact-Checking
item.description: arXiv:2604.16311v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Automated Fact-Checking (AFC) relies on claim extraction as a first step, yet existing methods largely overlook the multimodal nature of today's misinformation. Social media posts often combine short, informal text with images such as memes,...

item.title: Cross-Family Speculative Decoding for Polish Language Models on Apple~Silicon: An Empirical Evaluation of Bielik~11B with UAG-Extended MLX-LM
item.description: arXiv:2604.16368v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Speculative decoding accelerates LLM inference by using a small draft model to propose k candidate tokens for a target model to verify. While effective for same-tokenizer pairs on high-bandwidth GPUs, its applicability to cross-family pairs with...

item.title: Brain-CLIPLM: Decoding Compressed Semantic Representations in EEG for Language Reconstruction
item.description: arXiv:2604.16370v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Decoding natural language from non-invasive electroencephalography (EEG) remains fundamentally limited by low signal-to-noise ratio and restricted information bandwidth. This raises a fundamental question regarding whether sentence-level linguistic...

item.title: CFMS: Towards Explainable and Fine-Grained Chinese Multimodal Sarcasm Detection Benchmark
item.description: arXiv:2604.16372v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Multimodal sarcasm detection has recently garnered significant attention. However, existing benchmarks suffer from coarse-grained annotations and limited cultural coverage, which hinder research into fine-grained semantic understanding. To address...

item.title: Foundational Study on Authorship Attribution of Japanese Web Reviews for Actor Analysis
item.description: arXiv:2604.16376v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: This study investigates the applicability of authorship attribution based on stylistic features to support actor analysis in threat intelligence. As a foundational step toward future application to dark web forums, we conducted experiments using...

item.title: Some users may encounter issues with GPT-5.4-C model in Codex
item.description: Status: MonitoringWe have applied the mitigation and are monitoring the recovery.Affected components Codex API (Degraded performance) Codex Web (Degraded performance) CLI (Degraded performance) App (Degraded performance) VS Code extension (Degraded performance)

item.title: How to Ground a Korean AI Agent in Real Demographics with Synthetic Personas
[AINews] Moonshot Kimi K2.6: the world's leading Open Model refreshes to catch up to Opus 4.6 (ahead of DeepSeek v4?)
item.description: Yay Kimi!!!
